hotspots.title: Trump-Xi summit amid Iran war tensions
hotspots.blurb: President Donald Trump's high-stakes meeting with Chinese President Xi Jinping in Beijing has dominated coverage, with discussions focusing on trade, Taiwan, Iran, and AI. The summit is framed as critical for global stability amid escalating tensions in the Strait of Hormuz and the ongoing Iran war.
hotspots.affect: This commands disproportionate attention due to its geopolitical stakes, potential economic repercussions, and the symbolic weight of U.S.-China relations in a polarized global context.

hotspots.title: Nigel Farage’s £5m donation scandal
hotspots.blurb: Reform UK leader Nigel Farage faces a parliamentary investigation over an undeclared £5 million gift from a cryptocurrency billionaire. The scandal has sparked debates about transparency, political financing, and the integrity of UK electoral rules.
hotspots.affect: The scandal is highly charged due to its implications for political ethics, Farage's rising influence, and the broader debate over foreign funding in UK politics.

hotspots.title: Manuel Adorni’s illicit enrichment probe
hotspots.blurb: Argentina’s Chief of Cabinet Manuel Adorni is under investigation for alleged illicit enrichment, with his brother Francisco also charged. The scandal has intensified scrutiny of President Javier Milei’s government, raising questions about corruption and transparency.
hotspots.affect: The case is highly charged due to its implications for Milei’s anti-corruption platform, the government’s stability, and the broader public distrust of political elites in Argentina.

hotspots.title: Kylian Mbappé vs. France’s far-right
hotspots.blurb: French footballer Kylian Mbappé’s public criticism of the far-right National Rally (RN) has sparked a heated backlash from RN leaders Jordan Bardella and Marine Le Pen. The controversy has ignited debates about athletes’ political roles and the rise of far-right politics in France.
hotspots.affect: The clash is highly charged due to Mbappé’s celebrity status, the symbolic weight of his opposition to the far-right, and the broader cultural and political polarization in France.

hotspots.title: AfD’s poll surge and leadership ambitions
hotspots.blurb: Germany’s far-right Alternative für Deutschland (AfD) has surged in polls, particularly in eastern states like Mecklenburg-Vorpommern and Saxony-Anhalt. The party’s growing influence has sparked debates about its extremist ties, electoral strategies, and the broader rise of far-right politics in Europe.
hotspots.affect: The AfD’s rise commands disproportionate attention due to fears of democratic backsliding, historical parallels to fascism, and the party’s controversial stance on immigration and EU integration.
